unit.name: Why robot learning matters
unit.description: Motivates robot learning by contrasting it with supervised and self-supervised learning and by highlighting the recent surge of interest in general-purpose robots.
unit.summary: The lecture opens by contrasting supervised learning, which maps inputs to labels, and self-supervised learning, which extracts hidden structure from unlabeled data, with robot learning, where actions change the world and produce new observations and rewards [ts=96s] [ts=121s] [ts=157s]. Li emphasizes that robot learning is about choosing a sequence of actions to maximize reward or minimize cost rather than only predicting outputs [ts=193s]. He also frames the area as a fast-growing field, pointing to startup and industry momentum around robots that can physically interact with the environment [ts=205s] [ts=241s]. The segment ends with a roadmap spanning problem formulation, perception, reinforcement learning, model-based planning, imitation learning, foundation models, and remaining challenges [ts=279s] [ts=322s].

key_points.text: Robot learning differs from x-to-y prediction because actions change the environment and future observations.
The objective is to choose a sequence of actions that maximizes reward or minimizes cost.
Recent excitement comes from robots performing complicated physical tasks and attracting heavy investment.
The lecture roadmap covers formulation, perception, RL, model-based planning, imitation learning, foundation models, and challenges.

kp_catalog.name: Robot learning as interactive sequential decision-making
kp_catalog.description: Robot learning is distinguished from standard prediction problems because actions change the environment, generate new observations, and must be chosen over time to improve reward.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because robot learning as interactive sequential decision-making clarifies robot learning is distinguished from standard prediction problems because actions change the environment, generate new observations, and must be chosen over time to improve reward, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Goal-state-action-reward formulation
kp_catalog.description: Robot learning problems are structured around a task goal, current state, action choice, reward signal, and resulting next state.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because goal-state-action-reward formulation formalizes how robot learning problems are structured around a task goal, current state, action choice, reward signal, and resulting next state, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
